introduction:
The Ration Card in Uttar Pradesh is issued by the Department of Food and Civil Supplies, Government of Uttar Pradesh. It ensures food security to eligible resident households by providing subsidised and free food grains (wheat, rice) through the extensive network of Fair Price Shops (Kotedar).

UP leverages the National Food Security Act (NFSA) and strongly encourages an Aadhaar-driven Public Distribution System (PDS). Ration cards in UP act as critical proof of residence, economic status, and help citizens avail benefits under multiple state and central schemes, including free ration distributions.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Service charge implies only minor portal or Jan Seva Kendra (CSC) convenience fees (~₹30)
  • Processing time is roughly 30 to 45 days
  • The majority of new applications are successfully processed via Jan Seva Kendras
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all listed family members
  • Eldest female member of the family is made the Head of the Household
  • State Helpline: 1967 or 1800-1800-150
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for UP Ration Card

Note: Having a valid Income Certificate issued by the Revenue Department (Tehsil) is critical for defining the category (Patra Grihasti or Antyodaya).
Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card (Mandatory for all members)
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • MNREGA Job Card (Very beneficial in rural UP)
Address Proof
  • Recent Electricity or Water Bill
  • Bank Passbook (First page showing address)
  • Aadhaar Card (if having current UP address)
  • Gas Connection Book
Income & Other Documents
  • Income Certificate (Recent, issued by Tehsildar)
  • Passport-size photographs of the Head of the Family
  • Caste Certificate (if claiming specific category benefits)
Category Guide
  • Patra Grihasti (Eligible Household - PHH): Covers families below specified income limits under NFSA.
  • Antyodaya (AAY - Red Card): For the poorest families (e.g., landless laborers, marginalized groups). Provides highest subsidy.

Note: APL (Above Poverty Line) card concepts are mostly absorbed into the broad NFSA exclusions.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply via Jan Seva Kendra / e-District UP Portal

In Uttar Pradesh, direct online citizen applications without a CSC login can sometimes face hurdles. It is strongly recommended to apply online through an authorized Jan Seva Kendra (CSC).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Jan Seva Kendra
    Locate your nearest authorized Jan Seva Kendra / Lokvani Kendra. Carry all original documents.
  2. Operator Fills the Form
    The CSC operator logs into the UP e-District or FCS portal using their credentials. Provide details of the family head (preferably eldest female) and all family members.
  3. Document Upload
    The operator will scan your Aadhaar, Income Certificate, Address Proof, and Photograph and upload them to the system.
  4. Payment & Receipt
    Pay the nominal service fee to the operator (usually around ₹30). Ensure you collect the computer-generated Application Acknowledgement Number.
  5. Gram Panchayat / Supply Inspector Verification
    The application is forwarded to the Supply Inspector (SI) of your Tehsil / Block. A local physical verification will be conducted.
  6. Final Approval
    Once cleared, the Ration Card is digitized and added to the official NFSA beneficiary list. You can view it on the FCS portal.
Tip: An active mobile number and accurate Income Certificate are the most critical factors for speedy approval in UP.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / DSO Office

Citizens (especially in rural areas) can apply offline through the local administration.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Collect Application Form
    Visit your local Tehsil / Block Development Office (BDO) or the office of the District Supply Officer (DSO). Ask for the Ration Card Application Form.
  2. Fill in the Details
    Fill out the form manually. Details must match your Aadhaar exactly. Provide details of the nearest requested FPS (Kotedar).
  3. Attach Self-Attested Photocopies
    Attach copies of Aadhaar cards of all members, Income Certificate, and Address proof. Affix the family head's photo.
  4. Submit the File
    Submit the file at the designated counter at the Supply Office or to your Gram Pradhan (who forwards it). Take a receiving signature/stamp if possible.
  5. Verification
    A field verification is done by the Lekhpal/Gram Panchayat Adhikari and Supply Inspector.
  6. Card Generation
    Post verification, the data is fed into the system by the department operators. You will be mapped to a Fair Price Shop.
Processing Time: Offline processing tends to be slower, taking upwards of 45-60 days depending on the block backlog.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Search Name in NFSA List

Visit the official portal fcs.up.gov.in.
Click on "राशन कार्ड की पात्रता सूची" (Ration Card Eligibility List) to see if your name has been added under your district/block/Kotedar.

Download Digital Card (Parchi)

If your name is listed, you can view your digital ration card details (e-Ration Parchi). Provide this Parchi and Aadhaar at the Kotedar for rations.

Unit Addition/Deletion

Need to add a birth or remove a family member? This can be updated by again visiting a Jan Seva Kendra or the Supply Office with proofs.

Grievance Redressal (शिकायत)

Facing ration denial or delays? Use the "On-line Shikayat" feature on the FCS portal or call Toll-Free 1967.
